regression_risk: Branching from origin/<default> instead of local HEAD drops local-only commits not yet pushed; spec offers no deliberate opt-out to branch from local HEAD when a user has intentional unpushed work. Non-blocking ergonomic gap (the WARN-on-offline-fallback covers the offline case, not the deliberate-unpushed case).
summary: R4 design is sound. (1) Default-branch detection: the resolved Open Question (cheap `git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD` first, network `git remote show origin` fallback over the fetch R4 already performs) is robust. The symbolic-ref ref is only populated when remote HEAD was fetched/set-head ran, and the spec correctly designates the remote-show network call as the fallback that runs anyway, so a fresh worktree/CI checkout with an unset symbolic-ref still resolves; AC mandates 'detected, not hardcoded main'. Minor: spec doesn't explicitly say empty/garbage symbolic-ref output must fall through, but design intent is clear. (2) Fetch/offline: gated behind existing HasRemote() (gitops.go L225); any fetch failure falls back to local HEAD + WARN. Sound for a warn-and-continue design, though it folds offline/auth-error/no-remote into one fallback bucket -- acceptable because the fallback is always non-destructive, but spec should note the fallback fires on ANY fetch error so an auth failure doesn't hard-fail spec-create. (3) No ripple: DispatchBead (mindspec_executor.go L120-126) bases bead branches on workspace.SpecBranch(specID) by name, so changing the spec branch's base commit does not alter the bead-worktree flow. (4) Regression: real but non-blocking -- no opt-out to branch from local HEAD for deliberate unpushed work. (5) ADR-0030: properly mandated -- R4 Requirement, Design Decision, and ADR Touchpoints all bind the new fetch/detect/branch-from-remote helpers to the executor->gitutil seam (helpers land in internal/gitutil/gitops.go), command code never shells out raw.
